rules.title: Trip Rules
rules.subtitle: Please read the following rules carefully. These rules ensure a smooth, spiritually uplifting, and organized trip for everyone.
rules.rule1_desc: All men are required to join the congregation (Jama'at) for prayers at scheduled stops. Praying together strengthens the bonds of brotherhood, brings 27 times more spiritual reward, and anchors our trip in mindfulness and gratitude to Allah.
rules.rule2_desc: Playing music is strictly prohibited during the trip. Keeping the environment free of music allows everyone to relax, reflect on the beautiful Norwegian nature, listen to beneficial reminders, or converse with one another peacefully.
rules.rule3_desc: Protecting the honor of our fellow participants is essential. By strictly avoiding negative talk and backbiting, we keep our hearts clean, invite Allah's mercy, prevent drama, and ensure a stress-free environment for everyone.
rules.rule4_desc: Verbal complaining to other participants is prohibited as it dampens group morale. If you have any issues or concerns, they must be submitted in writing to the designated feedback person. This ensures problems are resolved constructively and professionally.
rules.rule5_desc: This trip is a chance to build lifelong bonds. We expect everyone to spread positivity, help one another, and show patience. These virtues elevate the experience from a simple vacation to a deeply uplifting and unforgettable journey.
rules.rule6_desc: Travel can be stressful — delays, tight spaces, and fatigue can test tempers. This rule encourages every participant to hold their composure and respond with patience and grace rather than frustration.
rules.rule6_quran: "Those who spend in prosperity and adversity, who repress anger, and who pardon people — Allah loves the doers of good." — Surah Aal-Imran 3:134
rules.rule7_desc: If Allah has fixed times for prayer, then respecting schedules is a form of discipline. When the bus departs at a set time, everyone must be present. One person's lateness affects all 17 travelers.
rules.rule7_quran: "Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers at specified times." — Surah An-Nisa 4:103
rules.rule8_desc: Norway's tourist areas will have mixed crowds. This rule reminds participants to maintain Islamic conduct and modesty in public spaces, preserving the spiritual atmosphere of the trip.
rules.rule8_quran: "Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their modesty. That is purer for them." — Surah An-Nur 24:30
rules.rule9_desc: Spend wisely on food, souvenirs, and extras. The trip has a shared budget, and individual extravagance can create tension or envy within the group. Be mindful and moderate in all spending.
rules.rule9_quran: "Eat and drink, but be not excessive. Indeed, He does not like those who commit excess." — Surah Al-A'raf 7:31
rules.rule10_desc: The Sprinter bus and shared accommodations must be kept clean. Each person is responsible for their own area. A tidy space keeps everyone comfortable and reflects our values.
rules.rule10_quran: "Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant and loves those who purify themselves." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:222
rules.rule11_desc: Express thanks — to Allah for the journey, to the organizers for planning, and to each other for companionship. Gratitude transforms the entire mood of the group and invites more blessings.
rules.rule11_quran: "If you are grateful, I will surely increase you [in favor]." — Surah Ibrahim 14:7
rules.rule12_desc: If you have nothing beneficial to say, it is better to stay silent. No harsh jokes, sarcasm, or hurtful remarks. Every word should build up, not tear down.
rules.rule12_quran: "And speak to people good words." — Surah Al-Baqarah 2:83
rules.sign_agree: I have read carefully and agree to uphold all rules

items.c1_desc: A high-quality windproof and waterproof jacket. Mountain weather is highly unpredictable.
items.c2_desc: Sturdy, waterproof trail boots with good ankle support for glacier and ravine stops.
items.c3_desc: Merino wool or warm base layers (top and bottom). Temperatures can drop below 8°C in passes.
items.c4_desc: Keeps feet warm and dry even if damp. Pack at least 3-4 pairs.
items.s1_desc: Lightweight, waterproof pocket prayer mat for praying in scenic outdoor locations.
items.s2_desc: A small spray bottle to easily perform wudu inside the Sprinter bus or on the road.
items.s3_desc: For daily spiritual recitations and travel reflections.
items.e1_desc: At least 15,000 mAh to keep your phone charged. GPS and camera drain battery quickly.
items.e2_desc: For keeping water or tea hot during long driving days.
items.e3_desc: Provides neck support during the multi-hour Sprinter minibus drives.
items.h1_desc: Crucial for the winding hairpin turns of Trollstigen and Eagle Road.
items.h2_desc: High-factor UV protection. Glaciers and snowy peaks amplify solar rays intensely.
items.h3_desc: For quick hygiene, cleaning hands on the road, and wudu cleanups.
items.c5_desc: Waterproof over-trousers for hiking in rain. Norwegian mountain weather changes fast.
items.c6_desc: Temperatures drop sharply at mountain passes and glacier viewpoints. Pack lightweight merino.
items.c7_desc: UV protection is essential near glaciers, snow, and open fjords. Polarised lenses recommended.
items.s4_desc: For finding prayer direction in remote mountain locations where signal may be weak.
items.s5_name: Dua Book / Fortress of the Muslim
items.s5_desc: Collection of daily duas and travel supplications. Essential for morning/evening adhkar.
items.e4_desc: Multi-port car charger for the Sprinter minibus. Keep all devices topped up during long drives.
items.e5_desc: Essential for evening prayers outdoors, cabin walks at night, and emergency situations.
items.h4_desc: Any prescription meds, inhalers, or EpiPens. Always carry in hand luggage, not checked bags.
items.h5_desc: Midges and mosquitoes near fjords and valleys are common in Norwegian summer.
items.h6_desc: Plasters, antiseptic wipes, paracetamol, ibuprofen, and blister pads for hiking.
items.d1_desc: Required for all participants. Keep accessible for hotel check-ins and emergencies.
items.d2_desc: European Health Insurance Card (EHIC) or travel insurance document. Essential for medical coverage.
items.d3_desc: Required if you're one of the designated drivers. Must be valid in Norway.
items.d4_desc: Have some Norwegian kroner (NOK) as backup. Not all mountain shops accept cards.
items.f1_desc: Stay hydrated on hikes and drives. Norway has excellent, clean tap water everywhere.
items.f2_desc: Dates, nuts, energy bars, and fruit for long driving stretches between stops.
items.f3_desc: For carrying essentials (water, snacks, prayer mat) during hikes and sightseeing stops.
items.f4_desc: For separating wet clothes, dirty laundry, or muddy shoes. Pack 5-10 bags.

finances.subtitle: Track your payments, trip costs, and verified budget evidence transparently.
finances.pending_payment_desc: Please transfer your outstanding share before departure.
finances.itemized_breakdown_desc: Click on any category below to expand itemized calculations, formulas, and verified official booking/pricing sources.
finances.math_proof_desc: We prove that the total group cost is distributed with absolute mathematical parity. The sum of all individual shares matches the group budget exactly:
finances.vipps_modal_desc: Since you are on a desktop browser, please scan this code or perform a manual transfer in the Vipps app.
finances.vipps_modal_scan_desc: Scan code from your phone's camera
